what_it_does: This file is a ledger terrain-entry that catalogues the ~/company directory as the 'Company spine', recording its identity, purpose, size, git remote, top-level registry folders, external connections, and the evidence used, while deliberately avoiding enumeration of its internals.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares type terrain-entry with register descriptive and status unconfirmed
Size is reported as 5.6G with a git remote of git@github.com:Concept-Vi/the-company.git
Contains links to [[foundation]] and [[company-cli]] and is launched-by [[company-systemd]]
The body explicitly defers reading internals to a separate future pass per orienteering/CLAUDE.md
Notes a minor size discrepancy between du (5.6G) and README/INDEX (5.7G) and attributes it to rounding
Tags are fabric, mcp, control; secrets is false; move_intent is none
standouts: It is intentionally a high-level boundary card, not an internal design document
Status is unconfirmed despite detailed observed evidence
It models the Company folder as the gravitational center that other ledger entries orbit
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with typed fields and wiki-style [[...]] cross-references
Follows a fixed section structure: What it is / How it works / What it connects to / When / where / Notes / evidence
Separates internal contents from external runtime/config dependencies by linking them as separate ledger entries
Annotates evidence as Read vs NOT read to mark scope boundaries
Maintains neutral, descriptive register without implementation detail
concerns: status is unconfirmed even though substantial evidence is listed
aliases repeats the name exactly, adding no information
The entry acknowledges it has not read the folder's internals, so completeness is limited by design
notes: A high-level orienteering card that treats ~/company as the central spine and maps what orbits it, deliberately leaving engine internals for a later pass.
questions: What does 'register: descriptive' mean in this ledger schema?
What value is intended for the empty kind field?
What does RHM stand for in the purpose statement?
When is the separate future pass for internals expected?
What is the exact total file count?
What is the specific meaning of the 'fabric' tag?
How does 'relation: company' differ from 'name: company'?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches as a high-level boundary/identity card: it names the Company spine and maps its orbit, but does not document the engine internals, which is consistent with its stated scope.
apparent_themes: Separation of spine identity from internal implementation
Evidence-based cataloguing with explicit scope limits
intention_signals: To establish a single authoritative ledger anchor for the Company folder, distinguishing what lives inside it from what lives outside it, and reserving internal details for a dedicated future pass.
novelty: It treats a home-directory project folder as a typed ledger 'terrain-entry' with explicit external-orbit relationships and a self-imposed ban on reading its own internals despite being the central spine.
contribution: Provides a stable high-level identity card and connection map for the Company folder, keeping spine-level context separate from both implementation internals and external runtime/config dependencies.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger terrain-entry for the ~/company directory, describing it as the Company spine (a typed compositional dataflow system with decisions/flows/runtime/MCP face), its size, git remote, top-level registry folders, and its connections to internal and external ledger entries.
intention_for_embedding: To create a high-level, evidence-based orienteering card that anchors the Company folder and defers its internals to a separate future pass.
